title: CivicSpark Microgrant
description: The CivicSpark Microgrant program provides small grants to support resident-led community projects across Toronto. Funding is intended to help grassroots groups organize activities such as community events, neighborhood improvement projects, local food initiatives, and other community-building programs.
eligibility: Toronto residents and grassroots community groups leading local community-building projects.
documents_required: Applicants may need to provide a short project description, budget outline, and community impact plan.
additional_notes: Supports small community initiatives led by residents. Funding amounts may vary depending on the project scope.

title: Incentives & Rebates to Green Your Home
description: The City of Toronto lists a selection of simple-low cost solutions, low-interest financing, and thousands of dollars in rebates to help you take advantage of incentives for making your home, business, or community more environmentally friendly. The City of Toronto offer support for: Home Energy Loan Program, Tower Renewal, Energy Retrofit Loans, Eco-Roof Incentive Program, Indigenous Climate Action Grants, Neighbourhood Climate Action Grants, Youth Climate Action Grants, PollinateTO Grants, Supports for Tree Planting on Private Property

title: Local Leadership Grant
description:
The Local Leadership Grant program provides $5,000 for resident-led community groups to help them inspire their neighbourhoods with events and activities that support the Toronto Strong Neighbourhoods Strategy. Projects must offer community-based activities related to at least one of the five domains of TSNS:

Economic Opportunities (e.g. job skills workshop),
Healthy Lives (e.g. wellness day),
Participation in Civic Decision-Making (e.g. community speak out event),
Physical Surroundings (e.g. community beautification event),
Social Development (e.g. neighbourhood festival),
eligibility: There is a range of eligibility criteria for this grant, a full list is available on the given link.
documents_required: Applicants must register for a Toronto Rebates, Incentives and Grants Portal (TGRIP) Account to apply for the Local Leadership Grant. Submissions will only be accepted through TGRIP.

title: Micro-grants
description: The Canada Service Corps (CSC) promotes civic engagement among young people from 15 to 30 years old through access to meaningful volunteer service placements that help them gain essential life skills and experience. Various Microgrant streams open up throughout the year, these are typically valued between $1,000 and $5,000.
eligibility: Eligibility criteria varies per microgrant, note that some Microgrants do not cover Ontario.

title: NETLIP and the NewTO App
organization: North East Toronto Local Immigration Partnership
description:
Since 2012, they have been enhancing the settlement and employment experiences of newcomers in the North Toronto community. They are now working alongside partners in Scarborough to build on existing accomplishments and strengthen support for communities across East Toronto.

NETLIP convenes a multi-sector Partnership Council focused on five key pillars: Health & Wellbeing; Employment & Labour Market; Settlement, Language & Integration; Technology & Digital Literacy; and Equity & Belonging—each supported by dedicated Action Groups that turn strategy into impact.

The NETLIP app provides support for finding housing, language services, community connections, education, health, and employment assistance.
